methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Fergani Space is a private-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Selçuk Bayraktar. The valuation universe comprises 5 publicly traded peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private comparables including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs—a total of 13 datapoints across the sector.

Peer-median valuation proves inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-stage filtering. While 25 same-sector peers exist, zero comparables satisfied simultaneous same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-tier criteria; the threshold for reliable peer medians is a minimum of three datapoints. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples is similarly non-viable: the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 42.9x revenue across the 5 public tickers, but Fergani Space's revenue is not disclosed—standard practice for private companies—rendering this multiple mechanically unusable. No triangulated valuation range can be derived from the available quantitative inputs.

Without disclosed revenue, a defined capital raise amount, or a sufficiently dense cluster of same-stage private comparables, conventional valuation methodologies cannot be reliably applied to Fergani Space. Meaningful valuation would require either: (1) target revenue guidance, (2) recent third-party funding round documentation, or (3) expansion of the peer set to adjacent aerospace verticals with greater data density at the private stage.
